input.title: Fitness and Gym Management System
input.description: Fitness and Gym Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React Native and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Express, MongoDB for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Member Registration
requirements: Register with personal info and fitness goals
Choose membership plan (monthly, yearly, pay-per-visit)
Payment for selected plan
Member ID card generation

FR-02.title: Workout Tracking
requirements: Log exercises with sets, reps, and weight
Pre-built workout templates
Track personal records per exercise
View workout history calendar

FR-03.title: Class Booking
requirements: View schedule of group fitness classes
Book spot in class with capacity limits
Cancel booking up to 2 hours before
Waitlist when class is full

FR-04.title: Personal Trainer Booking
requirements: Browse trainers with specialties and availability
Book one-on-one sessions
Trainer can set their schedule
Session reminders sent to both parties

FR-05.title: Nutrition and Meal Planning
requirements: Set daily calorie and macro targets
Log meals with food search database
Track daily intake vs targets
Meal plan suggestions based on goals

FR-06.title: Progress Photos and Measurements
requirements: Upload progress photos with date stamps
Log body measurements (weight, chest, waist, etc.)
Side-by-side photo comparisons
Progress charts over time

FR-07.title: Membership Management
requirements: View membership status and expiry
Renew or upgrade membership plan
Freeze membership temporarily
Cancellation with notice period

FR-08.title: Gym Admin Dashboard
requirements: View total members and active memberships
Revenue reports by plan type
Class attendance analytics
Equipment maintenance scheduling

FR-01.title: Plan Member Registration Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for member registration and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Register with personal info and fitness goals; Choose membership plan (monthly, yearly, pay-per-visit); Payment for selected plan.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for member registration is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for member registration are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for member registration can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Member Registration Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for member registration on React Native, Express, MongoDB. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Register with personal info and fitness goals; Choose membership plan (monthly, yearly, pay-per-visit); Payment for selected plan.
acceptance_criteria: Core member registration business rules are implemented for Fitness and Gym Management System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for member registration
API/service behavior for member registration is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Member Registration Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for member registration with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The member registration user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for member registration behave correctly
The member registration UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Member Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by member registration, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by member registration are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for member registration integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for member registration are handled securely

FR-01.title: Harden Member Registration Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to member registration. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for member registration are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in member registration are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for member registration passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Member Registration Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for member registration. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for member registration are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for member registration are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical member registration scenarios
